title: secretx-core: SecretUri::parse silently drops duplicate query params (last-wins)
description: What is unclear and for whom: SecretUri::parse inserts params into a HashMap, so if a URI has ?field=a&field=b, the second value silently wins. This is undocumented. A contributor testing URI parsing with duplicate params will observe surprising behavior without explanation. What would make it clear: add a doc comment on SecretUri::parse stating 'duplicate query parameter keys: the last value wins; earlier values are silently discarded.' Alternatively, return InvalidUri for duplicate keys (stricter). The current behavior is at least deterministic and consistent with most URL parsing libraries, so the documentation fix is sufficient.
